items.title: AI - Why it Matters?
items.deck: Why Traditional Market Skeptics Misread AI
items.html: 2008-style intuition doesnt transfer to GPU economics. Housing bubbles, bond convexity, and bank balance sheets live in one universe. AI lives in another: governed by physics scaling laws, and brutal engineering

items.title: All That Glitters Isn?t (Only) Gold
items.deck: Gold rebounds near $4,000 as traders reassess Fed cuts ? Bitcoin mirrors the same fear trade in digital form. What happens when both hedge the same risk?
items.html: Gold vs Bitcoin: Two 'insurance assets' in one macro moment. Gold steadies after a $3,800?$4,050 consolidation, while BTC toys with $100k ? both priced on faith in fiat fragility. Our charts track correlation flips, left-tail betas, and structurable hedges combining metals and crypto.

items.title: Rates say slowdown. Equities say party.
items.deck: 10y UST < 4%. Tech still euphoric. One of them is lying.
items.html: Bond market is quietly screaming "growth is fading". Stocks are still in 'soft landing forever' mode. This is the tension.

items.title: A 6?6 thought: why sum=7 feels special
items.deck: On two dice, the middle sum gets the most paths. Try highlighting the diagonal.
items.html: Count pairs, not magic. The six ways to make 7 line up as a diagonal in the 6?6 grid.

items.title: Kelly?s whisper
items.deck: If your edge is p, the sweet spot is roughly f = 2p ? 1. Too big kills you.
items.html: Play first in the lab, then open the derivation. $g(f)=p\ln(1+f)+(1-p)\ln(1-f)$ peaks at $2p-1$.
